tab_description: A system-generated ID to identify the upload files for each sub-module.

Name of the source where the data was taken from, e.g. "UBA Etox" or "EPA Ecotox" databases.
ID to identify the data entry at the source.
ID to identify the reference at the source.
Indicates whether the study is confidential or publically available.
URL or website, DOI, pdf-link to the NORMAN repository, etc.
Editor who created or modified the record.
Indication whether the study should be used in the derivation of quality targets
Date representing the day the record was created or modified.
Type of the reference of the source document: e.g. literature study, GLP study report, grey literature, Risk Assessment Report, etc.
A system-generated ID to identify the reference of the study.
Title of the publication
All authors names of the publication
Year of the publication
Journal or publisher name + city of publisher + Issue and pages
Tested compartment (matrix), e.g. freshwater, marine water, freshwater sediments, marine sediments, etc.
Indicates whether the reported value is exerimental or estimated.
Indicates whether the test is considered to be short term or long term.
Indicates whether the test represents a standard endpoint according to an OECD guideline.
??? INCHI KEY used in the NORMAN Database System (i.e. SUSDAT).
Substance name used in the study.
Substance CAS number used in the study.
EU number used in the study, in cases where no CAS exist.
Qualifier for the % purity value, e.g. >, =, <.
Reported purity of test material in %.
Source / Merchant, where the test material was purchased.
Name of vehicle ( i.e. solvent) used.
Was the concentration of the vehicle or other impurities reported? The vehicle should not exceed 0.01 %, i.e. to exert no effect.
Was the test substance radiolabeled? Which isotope was used? How was it quantified?
Was the method for preparing the stock solution reported?
Qualifier for the test standard used, e.g. "according, equivalent or similar to" the test guideline used.
Standard used, e.g. OECD Guideline 210 (Fish, Early-Life Stage Toxicity Test), or reference to a certain paper.

tab_h2: Principles of method if other than guideline
tab_description: Description of the test protocol if other than the guideline, giving a justification for using this method if appropriate.
Indicates whether the study is GLP compliant, e.g. yes, no, not reported.
Observed effect category, e.g. growth, reproduction, mortality.
Effect measurement, e.g. biomass vs growth rate, number of offspring or time to first breed, mortality, survival or immobilisation.
Endpoint type to which the effect concentration refers, e.g. LC50, LOEC, NOEC, EC10.
Molecular site within the test species which is affected by the toxicant.
Numeric value of the time until the effect is reported.
Unit of the test duration
Was recovery of test organism considered in the study design?
Scientific name of the test species.
Common name of the test species.
Taxonomic group or trophic level of test species: e.g. Algae and plants, Fish, Cyanobacteria, etc.
Body length of test species at the beginning of the experiment.
Body length of test species at the end of the experiment.
Unit of body length
Body weight of test species at the beginning of the experiment.
Body weight of test species at the end of the experiment.
Unit of body weight
Determination of cell concentrations or chlorophyll measurement at the begining of the experiment.
Determination of cell concentrations or chlorophyll measurement at the end of the experiment (for the control).
Indication whether any deformed or abnormal cells or organism are observed.
Reproductive condition of test species (number of offspring, mean coefficient of variation for section-by-section specific growth rate) according to minimum requirements.
Lipid content of the test species.
Age of the test species or inoculum.
Unit of age
Life stage of test species, e.g. Juvenile, adult, nymph, etc.
Gender of the test species.
Strain/clone of the test species.
Source, where test species were purchased. Is there information about the history of the control species? Have the test species been collected from a polluted site?
Information, how the test species culuture was handeled (conditions).
Did the test species have enough time to acclimate to the test conditions prior to the test begin?
Nominal concentrations or doses tested.
Unit of the nominal and measured concentrations
If the deviation from the nominal concentration is greater than 20 per cent, results should be based on measured concentrations
Indicator whether the experiment was a limit test.
Indicator whether the study was performed for range finding.
Indication whether a specific tissue or the whole organism is analyzed.
How often and when was the concentration determined?
Analytical method: description of method.
How was the recovery of the compound?
How was the limit of quantification?
Exposure regime (static, semistatic, flow through system,  change of water, repeated doses).
Exposure route, e.g. aqueous, diatary, injection.
Duration the test species were exposed to the test substance.
Unit of exposure duration
Duration the test was conducted for.
How often was the test material applied? Was the test solution renewed?
Unit of application frequency (if applicable)
Indicator whether a negative control was used?
Indicator whether a positive control was used?
Which substance was used as a positive control?
Were any effects observed in the control, e.g. high mortality.
Did the positive control show the expected effect?
Indicator whether a vehicle control was used?
Was the solvent control significant different from control?
How often was the water quality measured?
